cot_prefix: Okay, let's break this down. First, I need to understand what a "give and go" is in football (soccer). A give and go is a common attacking move where one player passes the ball to a teammate and then immediately runs towards the space where the ball will be, expecting the teammate to pass it back. This creates a quick exchange of possession and can be used to create space or penetrate the defense. Next, I need to consider who Toni Kroos is. Toni Kroos is a German professional footballer who plays as a midfielder for Real Madrid and the Germany national team. He is known for his precise passing, vision, and ability to control the tempo of the game. He is considered one of the best midfielders in the world and has won multiple UEFA Champions League titles and a FIFA World Cup with Germany in 2014. Now, let's think about whether it's plausible that Toni Kroos would perform a give and go. As a midfielder, Kroos is often involved in both building up play and transitioning from defense to attack. He is known for his short and long passing ability, and while he may not be as associated with quick, juggling dribbling moves as some other midfielders, he is certainly capable of making quick passes and moving into space to receive the ball. A give and go is a relatively simple but effective technique that any player with good passing ability and spatial awareness can execute. Kroos has both of these qualities in abundance. In fact, his ability to play one-touch passes and anticipate where the ball needs to go makes him well-suited for executing give and goes. I can imagine a scenario where Kroos receives the ball, quickly passes it to a teammate who is cutting in, and then immediately runs into the space that was just vacated by his teammate, expecting the ball to be passed back to him. This is exactly what a give and go entails. Therefore, the statement "Toni Kroos performed a give and go" is plausible. It suggests that Kroos, using his technical skills and spatial awareness, executed a quick one-two pass with a teammate, which is entirely within the realm of what a skilled midfielder like Kroos can do.
